reasoning: The FRED data for M1 (WM1NS) shows a slightly positive underlying trend in recent years as QT tapers, but with significant week-to-week volatility and clear seasonality. In 2024, the value increased slightly exactly 1 week after early June, supporting an initial leaning towards an increase (0.62). One month out in July 2024, the value dipped demonstrably below the June baseline, likely due to seasonal cash movements, justifying a forecast leaning downwards for July (0.41). Three months out (September) and six months out (December), the underlying positive drift and end-of-year holiday seasonality historically resulted in net increases above June baselines, placing the natural probabilities above 50%. As per the strict instructions to increase uncertainty over the forecast horizon, the absolute distances of probabilities from 0.5 are strictly kept monotonically decreasing for further dates: 0.12, 0.09, 0.06, and 0.03 respectively.
